claim_results.claim: Not because the answer is unclear — the answer has been sitting in plain view for sixteen centuries.
I have been afraid because the question is one of the most painful in Christianity, and most of the answers people give to it have made the pain worse.
I am going to take you to 1400 BC and make you stand in the dirt for a few minutes, because every honest answer to the OT/NT problem has to start there.

There is no hospital because there are no doctors, and there are no doctors because there is no germ theory and no antibiotics and no anesthesia and the closest thing to a medical professional in your village is the woman who knows which herbs to chew and which to spit out.

The marriage was arranged when the daughter was six, because a girl who is not betrothed by age ten in this world is a girl whose family has not secured an alliance, and a family without alliances is a family that is one bad harvest from extinction.
For most of Christian history, that claim has been an act of faith more than an act of reason.
Answer 1: The Atheist's — God Is Not Good The actions in the Old Testament are immoral by any standard you care to apply, therefore the God who commanded them is either evil, flawed, or fictional.
If "good" means "whatever God does," then the word "good" has no content.
What if all three could hold at once, in the same equation, in the same Person, without contradiction?
Earlier in this series we wrote down the equation that governs how a soul moves into or away from coherence with God.
Negentropic input that must be present for the equation to go anywhere except down.
Without $G$, the equation reduces to $dC/dt = -S \cdot C$, pure exponential decay.
Abolishing slavery instantly requires overriding the free will of every person currently engaged in it.

God refuses to do that, because the moment He does it for slavery He has established the principle that He will do it for any sufficiently bad behavior, and free will has been revoked from the species.
Circumstantial evidence was inadmissible.
I want to be careful here, because this is the passage where the framework's honesty matters most.
The pace is the place where the equation does its hardest work, because the pace is set by what the receivers can survive.
The $S \cdot C$ term is loud in the narrative because $S$ is what has to be named before it can be addressed.
"Take this." "Rest here." "Trust the procedure." The $G$ term comes forward in the narrative because $G$ is what the diagnosed patient needs to actually receive.
Same patient, same doctor, same disease, same equation.
If the framework is right, the OT/NT problem is not a contradiction that requires faith to ignore.
It is a single equation operating across two phases of one curriculum, taught by one Person, aimed at one outcome.
We have shown why with one equation: $dC/dt = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$.
It shows you why the action God took was the action that held all three constraints under conditions where every alternative violated at least one of them.
They are three terms in one equation that have to all be present for the equation to function.
The Canaanite child sacrifice claim is real but its scope is debated.
"Love your neighbor" as graduation, not orientation, is a strong hermeneutic claim.
It is not — it is a claim about how the teaching becomes intelligible, not about its importance.
The math shows that a specific equation with three terms can hold three constraints simultaneously.
The inference requires several additional steps, including the identification of justice with $S \cdot C$, mercy with $G$, and free will with $O$.
Every model is a projection of higher-dimensional reality onto a lower-dimensional surface we can comprehend.
We do not claim to have captured God in equations.
We claim that when we look at His creation honestly — with the tools of physics and the revelation of Scripture — the same structure appears in both.
Where our model limits what God can be, the limitation is ours, not His.
Status: DRAFT — three-constraint model applied to OT/NT problem; historical anchoring via Bronze Age bioarchaeology

CLAIM: The apparent difference between OT God and NT God is entirely explained by the presence or absence of the Holy Spirit distribution mechanism, which changes what the adversary can do, which changes what God must do in response.
The pattern: God intervenes externally and catastrophically because there is no internal correction mechanism.
Bioarchaeological evidence from sites such as Amarna (Akhetaten) provides a window into a population under constant physiological stress.

The movement of a soul or a society toward or away from alignment can be represented as: Coherence Equation — Civilizational Application $\frac{dC}{dt} = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$ The $S \cdot C$ term represents structural justice.
The mathematical reality of the coherence equation suggests that a system cannot optimize for $S$ (entropy) indefinitely.
The equation does not care that we replaced bronze statues with clinic waiting rooms; it only cares that the output of the system is the sacrifice of the future for the sake of a counterfeit present.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.

The angry-God-of-the-OT / loving-God-of-the-NT paradox dissolves when you recognize that the same coherence equation, $dC/dt = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$, contains all three contested terms â€” justice ($S \cdot C$), mercy ($G$), and free will ($O$) â€” and they must all hold simultaneously.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.

Kill if: Bronze Age bioarchaeology fails to support the cited mortality and disease numbers (~48.8% under-15 mortality, schistosomiasis up to 65%, life expectancy 25â€“30), or the Phoenician tophet evidence for Canaanite child sacrifice is shown to be substantially overstated by prophetic polemic.

Kill if: The three-constraint model fails â€” i.e., a hard OT action exists that cannot be parsed as God holding justice ($S \cdot C$), mercy ($G$), and free will ($O$) simultaneously, or the Cross fails to show all three terms operating in one act.
If the framework requires sacrificing one constraint to explain the text, the OT/NT continuity claim falls.
Every equation referenced in the paper, collected as a single index.
Plain English: the same equation that governs a single soul also governs a civilization â€” growth is multiplicative ($O$ and $G$ both required), decay is automatic.
Pure Decay (no openness) $$\frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C$$ Plain English: when $O = 0$, the equation reduces to exponential decay.
The article’s claim is that grace was never withdrawn even in the flood, conquest, or exile â€” but without openness on the human side, the system has nothing to grow on.

$$\underbrace{S \cdot C}_{\text{Justice}} \;\;\; \underbrace{G}_{\text{Mercy}} \;\;\; \underbrace{O}_{\text{Free Will}}$$ Plain English: the apparent contradiction between justice, mercy, and free will is resolved structurally â€” the three live as separate terms in one equation.
Cited as evidence that the narrative tracks a real entropic process, not a numerological pattern.
$$\frac{dC_\text{society}}{dt} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C) - S \cdot C$$ Plain English: the same coherence equation applied at population scale.
The negentropic energy without which the equation only decays.
Framework note: Entropy (physical frame) and Sin (theological frame) name the same dynamical quantity at the master-equation level.
The claim that justice, mercy, and free will are three terms in one equation, not three competing values.
NT â€” same patient, same doctor, same disease, same equation.

Logos Field ($\chi$) The pervasive substrate of coherent reality — the framework's $\chi$ field; the Master-Equation field whose value is total system coherence. (Earlier drafts denoted this with $\Lambda$; that symbol is now reserved for the cosmological constant.) Field ≠ coupling : this entry locks the substrate sense.
